message.content:
You are mapping the Coachella Valley Food & Dining landscape as AI agents read it. Your specific angle: Query "fine dining Coachella Valley", "high-end restaurants Palm Springs", "tasting menu desert", "best steakhouse Coachella Valley". Focus on upper-tier independents and resort-anchored fine dining.

Query the web extensively for this angle. Use WebSearch (load via ToolSearch: query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (load via ToolSearch: query "WebFetch") for live source-reading. Run multiple searches at different framings — vary the wording to surface different result sets.

The Coachella Valley = nine incorporated cities: Palm Springs, Desert Hot Springs, Cathedral City, Rancho Mirage, Palm Desert, Indian Wells, La Quinta, Indio, Coachella — plus adjacent communities (Bermuda Dunes, Thousand Palms, Mecca, Thermal).

For each establishment found, return:
- name (exact, as it appears in primary sources)
- city (one of the nine above, or "Adjacent Communities")
- cuisine_or_type
- why_mentioned (e.g., "consistently appears on Eater best-of list", "James Beard semifinalist 2024", "longtime independent operating since 1985")
- sources (URLs of pages where this surfaced — at least 1, prefer 2-3)

Be inclusive. The map's job is completeness, not curation. Surface:
- Dominant names that appear on best-of lists
- Notable independents
- Longtime / multi-generation places
- Underpowered places that earn their spot by reputation despite weak online presence
- Anchor-reference entities (chains, resort-owned, franchises) where they genuinely shape the landscape

Aim for ~12 entities. Quality of identification matters — downstream dedupe handles overlap.

Discipline: do not invent. If a "James Beard winner" is being claimed, confirm via primary source before listing it. Mention only what you can substantiate.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about high-end restaurants in Palm Springs with fine dining and tasting menus:

## Fine Dining Highlights in Palm Springs

Palm Springs is home to an impressive array of fine dining establishments that offer exceptional culinary experiences for the most discerning palates.

### Restaurants with Tasting Menu Options

Bar Cecil offers a chef's tasting menu featuring a three-course meal that ends with their incredible pear sticky toffee pudding.

### Notable Fine Dining Establishments

Several high-end restaurants stand out in Palm Springs:

- **Le Vallauris**: A cornerstone of fine dining in Palm Springs offering elegant French cuisine with classic French dishes expertly prepared with the finest ingredients, renowned for its impeccable service and attention to detail.

- **4 Saints**: Located on the rooftop of the Kimpton Rowan Hotel with breathtaking views of the San Jacinto Mountains, featuring globally inspired dishes crafted with locally sourced ingredients.

- **Willie's Modern Fare**: Combines the elegance of fine dining with classic cuisine with a modern twist, along with creative cocktails and carefully curated wine list.

- **Spencer's Restaurant**: Terraced at the foot of San Jacinto Mountain and overlooking Palm Springs, offering a variety of dining venues from terraced gardens to a fire-lit patio.

- **Johannes**: Celebrated for its innovative Austrian cuisine with a contemporary twist, featuring minimalist decor and intimate atmosphere that complements the culinary artistry.

From exceptional steakhouses and charming wine bars to outstanding international fare, Greater Palm Springs offers fine dining experiences that are truly enchanting and unforgettable.

## Top Steakhouse Recommendations:

**Mastro's Steakhouse Palm Desert**
As an OpenTable Diners' Choice winner for 2025, Mastro's Palm Desert continues to be a top destination for unforgettable dining in the Coachella Valley. Mastro's offers nightly live entertainment with top local musicians performing every evening, and valet parking and complimentary self-parking are available.

**Eddie V's Prime Seafood**
Eddie V's - Palm Desert is a top-tier destination for steak enthusiasts, offering a remarkable Steakhouse experience with prime center-cut steaks that are broiled to perfection. With an award-winning wine selection curated by an Advanced Sommelier, the impeccable service and vibrant ambiance make Eddie V's a must-visit for an unforgettable steak dining experience in Palm Desert.

**Sullivan's Steakhouse**
You can look forward to hand-cut steaks, fresh seafood, signature cocktails, and lively music at this one-of-a-kind steakhouse located in the Shops and Gardens of El Paseo.

**The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente Rancho Mirage**
The restaurant has been the winner of the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007 and has been Forbes Four-Star rated for six consecutive years, and also earned the AAA Four Diamond Award.

These establishments offer upscale dining experiences with quality steaks, seafood, and exceptional service throughout the Coachella Valley.

I've found comprehensive information about upscale restaurants in Palm Springs. Here are some of the best options:

## Fine Dining & Upscale Restaurants

Popular upscale choices include Copley's on Palm Canyon, The Colony Club at Colony Palms, Le Vallauris, Spencer's Restaurant, Workshop Kitchen + Bar, Mr. Lyons Steakhouse, Bar Cecil, 4 Saints, Melvyn's, and The Steakhouse, showcasing the refined side of Palm Springs dining with everything from romantic courtyard dinners to contemporary chef-driven tasting menus.

**Notable Highlights:**

- **4 Saints**: An upscale restaurant with one of the best brunches around, featuring amazing dishes and a great view of Palm Springs.

- **Le Vallauris**: One of the finest culinary destinations in Palm Springs, located in a historic ranch-style house and specializing in five-star dining with upscale dishes like wild boar pate, Maine lobster ravioli and seared whitefish with mustard sauce.

- **Sandfish Sushi Bar**: Head Chef Engin Onural opened this sushi joint with stunning design, offering an upscale dining experience for Palm Springs.

- **Copley's on Palm Canyon**: Set in the former Cary Grant estate, it exudes old Hollywood glamour with contemporary American cuisine, multiple dining areas including intimate indoor rooms and outdoor patio with gardens and mountain views, with chef Mark Van Laanen creating elevated dishes using locally sourced ingredients.

- **Bar Cecil**: One of the tougher-to-book spots in town with reservations getting nabbed weeks in advance, even during the low season.

These restaurants represent some of Palm Springs' most prestigious dining destinations with exceptional service and cuisine.

Links: [{"title":"Did you know…about Le Vallauris | Le Vallauris Restaurant","url":"https://levallaurisrestaurant.wordpress.com/2013/10/15/did-you-know-about-le-vallauris/"},{"title":"Le Vallauris","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/listing/le-vallauris/72/"},{"title":"Le Vallauris Restaurant - Palm Springs, CA | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/le-vallauris"},{"title":"Le Vallauris Restaurant - Palm Springs","url":"https://casago.com/palm-springs/restaurants/le-vallauris-restaurant/"},{"title":"LE VALLAURIS, Palm Springs - Menu, Prices, Restaurant Reviews & Reservations - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32847-d378387-Reviews-Le_Vallauris-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"PalmSprings.com Spotlight: Le Vallauris - Palm Springs","url":"https://www.palmsprings.com/palmsprings-com-spotlight-le-vallauris/"},{"title":"Le Vallauris | Soho House","url":"https://www.sohohouse.com/en-us/restaurants/le-vallauris"},{"title":"Palm Springs History – Le Vallauris Restaurant","url":"https://levallaurisrestaurant.wordpress.com/category/palm-springs-history/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Le Vallauris:

## Founding and History

Le Vallauris was founded in 1974 by Paul Bruggemans and occupies the former home of George Roberson, son of Nellie Coffman, the visionary owner of the famed Desert Inn. The restaurant is housed in the 1927 Roberson home, tucked up behind downtown Palm Springs.

## Background and Inspiration

Bruggemans and Camille Bardet previously owned Hollywood's celebrated Le St. Germain Restaurant (which closed in 1988), and it wasn't difficult to pay homage to that establishment's luxurious intimacy with Le Vallauris.

## Restaurant Character

Rather than being a typical formal French establishment, the restaurant exudes the atmosphere of a cheerful, casual French cafe with seasoned waiters fluent in French and dressed in impeccable suits, with a backdrop of Flemish tapestries and Louis-XV furniture. Bruggemans wisely chose to keep the historic house intact with its original layout, including its expansive patio shaded by fichus trees Bruggemans planted himself, now dotted with clusters of soft beehive lights.

## Recognition

Le Vallauris is the Desert's most award-winning 5-star restaurant, with Zagat awarding it their highest rating of "Extraordinary to Perfection" in all categories for Food, Decor and Service.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Workshop Kitchen + Bar in Palm Springs and its James Beard Award:

Workshop Kitchen + Bar, located in the historic El Paseo Building on North Palm Canyon in Palm Springs, became Palm Springs' first James Beard award winner for Outstanding Restaurant Design in the category of over 75 seats. The restaurant won an "Outstanding Restaurant Design, 76 Seats & Over" award in 2015 by the James Beard Foundation.

Helmed by Chef/Owner Michael Beckman, along with partner Joseph Mourani, the 2015 James Beard Award winning restaurant features a market-driven menu comprised of seasonal food highlights harvested from local farms. The almost-100-year-old building features soaring 27-foot ceilings and poured concrete in every direction, from the long communal table spanning the entire room to the side booths, with hanging silver light fixtures and touches of black adding warmth and depth.

Additionally, the restaurant is a 4-time Michelin recommended establishment.

## Overview

Morgan's in the Desert is La Quinta's signature dining venue showcasing contemporary American cuisine inspired by the bounty of the Coachella Valley. The restaurant is spearheaded by celebrated chef/restaurateur Jimmy Schmidt and evokes the glamour, history and architecture of the original 1926 venue.

## Cuisine & Menu

The restaurant showcases contemporary American cuisine inspired by the bounty of the Coachella Valley, sourcing the region's top products and crafting them with traditional cooking methods to bring deliciously simple and healthy dishes. Offerings include classic American Rib Eye steak to the signature Tiramisu Bomb.

## Dining Experience

Morgan's in the Desert is a fine dining contemporary American restaurant. The outdoor patio seating with heaters and fire pits is highlighted for its ambiance at sunset and during cool evenings. There is live music Wednesday through Sunday from 7-11 PM.

## Hours & Reservations

Morgan's is open for dinner from 5:30 to 10 PM daily, with bar hours from 5-10 and until midnight on Friday and Saturday. Reservations are highly recommended.

## Contact Information

The restaurant is located at 49-499 Eisenhower Drive, La Quinta, CA 92253, with phone number (760) 564-5700.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Melvyn's, the Ingleside Inn, and its connection to Frank Sinatra:

## Restaurant History

Melvyn's opened its doors in 1975 at the historic Ingleside Estate, under the vision of Mel Haber. Mel Haber bought the former estate on a handshake for $300,000, and spent around $500,000 restoring the property.

## The Historic Property

When Mel Haber arrived in Palm Springs in 1975, he discovered a neglected estate on the edge of downtown—the Ingleside Inn, a Spanish Colonial Revival retreat dating back to 1922. Originally built as a private estate for the Birge family of the Pierce-Arrow Motor Car Company, it had once been an exclusive hideaway for the elite under Ruth Hardy's ownership.

## Frank Sinatra Connection

Icons such as Frank Sinatra, Marlon Brando, Marilyn Monroe, Elizabeth Taylor, and members of the Rat Pack frequented the restaurant, helping cement its reputation as a cultural hub of Palm Springs nightlife and fine dining.

Sinatra's connection was particularly memorable: he even held a rehearsal dinner at Melvyn's for his wedding in 1976, further intertwining the restaurant with Hollywood lore. More specifically, the dinner party was the rehearsal dinner party for his wedding to Barbara Marx.

## Sinatra's Preferences

His go-to orders included veal with vegetables and the now-famous Steak Diane, which came with a splendidly retro tableside presentation, complete with a roaring flame of cognac sauce. He was known to tip generously, handing $100 bills to staff members when he arrived.

Based on the search results, I found information about Johannes, the Palm Springs Austrian restaurant and its chef:

Johannes Bacher, known as "The Eclectic Chef," was born and raised in a small mountain village in Tirol, Austria, and is the Executive Chef/Owner of Johannes Restaurant in Palm Springs, CA.

Chef Johannes Bacher's global modern European award-winning cuisine, including his popular fan favorite "Wiener Schnitzel," has been a downtown Palm Springs staple since 1999.

Inspired at age 7 by his mother to start cooking, Chef Johannes developed a passion for food that spanned decades of worldwide travel and classical training, and among his accolades is being the Corporate Chef of Seabourn Cruise Line for 5 years.

Known as "The Eclectic Chef," Bacher combines his heritage with global culinary expertise to create a dining experience that is both sophisticated and approachable. The restaurant is located at 196 S. Indian Canyon Drive in downtown Palm Springs.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Edge Steakhouse at the Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage:

## Overview
The Edge, with sweeping views of Rancho Mirage, crafts remarkable wagyu steaks, both premium hand-cut and bone-in meats as well as seafood paired with an international wine list unrivaled in California's Greater Palm Springs Valley.

## Wagyu Offerings
The restaurant offers Satsuma Japanese Wagyu from Kagoshima Prefecture on the island of Kyushu in Japan. Additionally, The Edge offers prime-cut steaks including Japanese and Australian Wagyu beef, hand-cut and dry-aged bone-in meats, and seafood.

## Beef Program
Except for their wagyu, they get everything from an Amish farm in Pennsylvania. The Edge features its own dry-aging room, where select cuts of grass-fed, corn-finished beef are aged 21-65 days.

## Location & Ambiance
The restaurant pairs premium steaks and pristine seafood with sweeping views of the Coachella Valley, creating a setting that feels both elevated and immersive. The restaurant is open Wednesday through Sunday from 5:00 p.m. to 10:00 p.m. (final seating at 9:00 p.m) and has a smart casual dress code.

Based on the search results, I found information about Copley's on Palm Canyon and Cary Grant, but I should note that the chef's name in your query appears to be different from what the sources indicate.

Copley's On Palm Canyon opened its doors in December 2004 and quickly became one of the premier eateries in Palm Springs, garnering both local and national recognition. Located in the heart of Palm Springs, the 1940's former Cary Grant guesthouse has been converted into Copley's Restaurant, a casual yet sophisticated restaurant offering a friendly indoor and outdoor unique dining experience.

Copley's, which is named after its England-born executive chef, Andrew Manion Copley, first opened its doors in December 2004. Executive Chef Andrew Copley has an extensive 20-year culinary background at five-star restaurants and hotels across the world, and is well known for innovative dishes such as Asian Spiced Barramundi, Charred Berkshire Pork Chop and creative nightly specials.

The search results do not contain any reference to a chef named "Mark Van Laanen" in connection with Copley's on Palm Canyon. The executive chef is Andrew Copley (full name Andrew Manion Copley).

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Bar Cecil in Palm Springs:

## Chef and Leadership

Bar Cecil's culinary team is led by partner-Executive Chef Gabriel Woo, former executive chef of Sparrow's Lodge and Holiday House in Palm Springs, and a 2019 guest chef at James Beard House in New York City. Chef Woo, a Palm Springs native, skillfully incorporates produce from San Diego's Girl & Dug Farm and locally grown dates onto the seasonally inspired, French-leaning menu.

## Reservations

Since opening in 2021, Bar Cecil has been one of the most popular reservations in Palm Springs, if not the most difficult one in the greater Coachella Valley. Online reservations open at exactly 8 am PST 2 weeks in advance. If you can't secure a reservation, your best bet is to arrive before doors open and put your name down for a coveted bar seat.

## Recognition and Atmosphere

Bar Cecil is a MICHELIN restaurant. The intimate 75-seat restaurant's name and colorful, maximalist interior design are inspired by Sir Cecil Beaton, the late British photographer, designer and all-around queer icon, whose portrait hangs on the wall by the bar.

## Food and Drink

Bar Cecil serves exceptional yet familiar bistro fare, ice-cold martinis and incredible atmosphere. The classically inclined drink program includes several variations on a martini, including a supersized $50 version listed under the cheekily labeled "Why Not?" section of the menu.

The Steakhouse is a recipient of the prestigious Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star award and winner of the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007, and it is located at the Agua Caliente Resort Casino Spa in Rancho Mirage, California.

The restaurant has been Forbes Four-Star rated for six consecutive years, and has earned additional accolades such as the AAA Four Diamond Award, the Palm Springs Life Best of the Best, and the OpenTable Diners' Choice Award.

The Steakhouse is the only Forbes Four-Star rated restaurant in the Coachella Valley and is one of only 113 in the world.

The restaurant features fine dining with the finest beef and chops, premium seafood and unparalleled wine selection, and offers amenities including a private dining room, inside patio and a gorgeous bar. Sunday Brunch is also available from 10 am - 2 pm.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about 4 Saints at Kimpton Rowan Palm Springs:

## Overview
4 Saints is a rooftop restaurant at the Kimpton Rowan Palm Springs Hotel. It's a Michelin-recommended restaurant offering a refined dining experience with stunning views of Palm Springs, the Coachella Valley, and the San Jacinto Mountains.

## Location & Access
The restaurant is located on the 7th Floor at 100 W Tahquitz Canyon Way, Palm Springs, CA 92262, and can be reached at (760) 392-2020.

## Cuisine & Menu
The modern American fare is guided by the bounty of California's ranches, farms, vines, and coastlines, with small and large plates showcasing pristine seafood, heirloom local vegetables, and prime meats. The menu is seasonal and Mediterranean-inspired, crafted with fresh, locally sourced ingredients.

## Atmosphere & Views
The restaurant features cool 270-degree views and warm hospitality. Floor-to-ceiling windows and outdoor terrace seating create a stylish backdrop, perfect for romantic dinners, special celebrations, or sunset cocktails.

## Hours
Operating hours are Monday–Thursday 5 PM–9 PM, Friday–Saturday 5 PM–10 PM, and Sunday 5 PM–9 PM, with reservations highly recommended.

Links: [{"title":"WALLY’S DESERT TURTLE - Updated May 2026 - 468 Photos & 515 Reviews - 71775 Highway 111, Rancho Mirage, California - Steakhouses - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/wallys-desert-turtle-rancho-mirage"},{"title":"Wally's Desert Turtle | RESTAURANTS, CATERING, FOOD & BEVERAGE","url":"https://business.ranchomiragechamber.org/list/member/wally-s-desert-turtle-196"},{"title":"About — WALLY'S DESERT TURTLE","url":"https://www.wallysdesertturtle.com/about"},{"title":"An Inside Look at the Historic Wally's Desert Turtle in Rancho Mirage","url":"https://localemagazine.com/historic-fine-dining-restaurants-in-rancho-mirage/"},{"title":"Wally's Desert Turtle :: Best American in Rancho Mirage","url":"http://www.wallysdesertturtle.net/"},{"title":"The Way We Were | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/wallys-desert-turtle-rancho-mirage/"},{"title":"Best Restaurants in Rancho Mirage: Wally's Desert Turtle","url":"https://localemagazine.com/best-restaurants-in-rancho-mirage/"},{"title":"Wally's Desert Turtle | Vacation Palm Springs","url":"https://vacationpalmsprings.com/restaurant/wallys-desert-turtle"}]

Based on the search results, here's the history of Wally's Desert Turtle:

## Founding and Early Years

Wally's Desert Turtle in Rancho Mirage, CA was established in 1978 by Wally Botello, the founder of the Velvet Turtle chain. Wally had founded The Velvet Turtle group of fine-dining restaurants in 1965 while living in Los Angeles, sold the chain and moved to the desert in the early 1970s.

With a little help from renowned designer Steven Chase and the restaurant's original French chef, Jean-Louis Jalouneix, Botello's vision came true to create a five-star dining experience right in the heart of Rancho Mirage.

## Ownership Transition

After Wally's passing in 1985, his son Michael Botello took the reins, nurturing the restaurant into a treasured hidden gem within the valley. The third generation is now guiding this esteemed institution, with Michael's daughter, Madalyn Botello, at the forefront of the family legacy, and she continues to serve cherished patrons while welcoming a new generation of guests.

## Notable Features

Wally's has been open since 1978 and has been in the same family for three generations. Wally's is known for its Imported Dover Sole, Rack Of Lamb and decadent Soufflés. For over forty-five years, Wally's Desert Turtle has been honored to host a distinguished array of celebrities, world dignitaries, and esteemed guests, including Bob and Dolores Hope, Frank Sinatra, President Gerald R. and Mrs. Betty Ford, Benjamin Netanyahu, and Tom Hanks.

Links: [{"title":"SPENCER’S RESTAURANT - Updated June 2026 - 1852 Photos & 1900 Reviews - 701 W Baristo Rd, Palm Springs, California - New American - +17603273446 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/spencers-restaurant-palm-springs-2"},{"title":"Spencer's Restaurant","url":"https://www.spencersrestaurant.com/"},{"title":"Spencer’s Restaurant | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/spencers-restaurant-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Spencer's Restaurant - Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://www.opentable.com/spencers-restaurant"},{"title":"Spencer's Restaurant - Palm Springs California American Restaurants","url":"https://directory.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/spencers/"},{"title":"About","url":"https://www.spencersrestaurant.com/about/"},{"title":"Spencer's Restaurant - Palm Springs, California - Gil's Thrilling (And Filling) Blog","url":"https://www.nmgastronome.com/?p=44813"},{"title":"About Spencer's Restaurant for Fine American, European, and Pacific Rim Dining in Palm Springs California","url":"https://www.restauranteur.com/spencerspalmsprings/aboutus.htm"},{"title":"Spencer's Restaurant | Vacation Palm Springs","url":"https://vacationpalmsprings.com/restaurant/spencers-restaurant"},{"title":"Spencer's Restaurant | Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/spencers-restaurant/15105/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Spencer's Restaurant in Palm Springs:

## History and Background

Spencer's Restaurant was established in 2000. It is set at the historic Palm Springs Tennis Club, an exclusive gathering place since its inception in the 1930s. The Tennis Club has been an exclusive gathering place for celebrities such as Katherine Hepburn and Bob Hope.

## Restaurant Concept

Spencer's offers a popular Palm Springs tradition of culinary excellence featuring a fresh approach to classic American, European and Pacific Rim cuisine. The restaurant is named after owner Harold Matzner's award-winning 110-pound Siberian husky.

## Acclaim and Recognition

Spencer's exquisite atmosphere has garnered national acclaim and was named one of the "100 Best Brunch and Alfresco Restaurants in America" by OpenTable diners. Locally, it has been voted Best Sunday Brunch, Best Outdoor Dining, Best Power Lunch, Best Wine List, Best Chef, Best Caterer and Most Romantic.

I found search results about Sandfish, Engin Onural, and Palm Springs sushi. Here's what the search revealed:

Sandfish Sushi and Whiskey has been serving modern sushi delicacies in uptown Palm Springs since 2018, with chef and founder Engin Onural drawing cultural influences from his world travels to create a unique dining experience.

The restaurant is described as an award-winning sushi establishment and creative sushi & Japanese Whiskey bar. The spot is notable for paying homage to Japan's whiskey tradition rather than the typical sake focus at other sushi restaurants.

Engin Onural earned his Bachelor of Science in Business Information Management from Bilkent University in Ankara, Turkey, before moving to Los Angeles to train at the Sushi Chef Institute and become a certified sake sommelier. In 2011, Engin opened The Venue Sushi Bar & Sake Lounge in Palm Desert, California, which was voted "Best Sushi in Coachella Valley" by Palm Springs Life Magazine every year.

However, the search results do not contain specific information about Sandfish receiving a Michelin star or rating.

content.text: I have substantial material. Let me compile the StructuredOutput with at least 12 entities.

entities.name: Le Vallauris
entities.why_mentioned: Cornerstone of Palm Springs fine dining since 1974, founded by Paul Bruggemans (formerly of Hollywood's Le St. Germain); housed in the 1927 Roberson home; widely described as the desert's most award-winning 5-star restaurant with top Zagat ratings; appears on virtually every Coachella Valley fine-dining best-of list

entities.name: Melvyn's Restaurant & Bar
entities.why_mentioned: Longtime Palm Springs institution at the Ingleside Inn, opened 1975 by Mel Haber; longtime Rat Pack and Frank Sinatra hangout (Sinatra held his 1976 rehearsal dinner here); signature tableside Steak Diane; recently marked 50th anniversary

entities.name: Workshop Kitchen + Bar
entities.why_mentioned: Won the 2015 James Beard Foundation Award for Outstanding Restaurant Design (76 Seats & Over) — Palm Springs' first James Beard win; four-time Michelin recommended; chef/owner Michael Beckman

entities.name: Bar Cecil
entities.cuisine_or_type: French-leaning bistro / cocktail bar with chef's tasting menu
entities.why_mentioned: Michelin Guide listed; widely cited as the hardest reservation in the Coachella Valley since opening 2021; partner-Exec Chef Gabriel Woo (Palm Springs native, 2019 James Beard House guest chef); appears on Infatuation, Time Out, and Eater-adjacent best-of lists

entities.name: Copley's on Palm Canyon
entities.why_mentioned: Set in Cary Grant's former 1940s guesthouse; opened December 2004 by England-born Executive Chef Andrew Manion Copley; cited on multiple regional best-of lists as a defining upscale Palm Springs dining destination

entities.name: Johannes
entities.cuisine_or_type: Modern European with Austrian/Asian fusion accents
entities.why_mentioned: Downtown Palm Springs staple since 1999; chef/owner Johannes Bacher (Tirol, Austria-born), former Seabourn Cruise Line corporate chef; consistently cited in regional fine-dining roundups

entities.name: Spencer's Restaurant
entities.cuisine_or_type: American / European / Pacific Rim fine dining
entities.why_mentioned: Opened 2000 at the historic 1930s Palm Springs Tennis Club; owned by Harold Matzner; named to OpenTable's '100 Best Brunch and Alfresco Restaurants in America'; locally awarded Best Wine List, Best Power Lunch, Most Romantic

entities.name: 4 Saints
entities.why_mentioned: Coachella Valley's only rooftop fine-dining restaurant, on the 7th floor of the Kimpton Rowan; Michelin-recommended; called out in Palm Springs Life and multiple regional roundups

entities.name: Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey
entities.why_mentioned: Chef Engin Onural's uptown Palm Springs flagship since 2018; named in upscale-dining roundups; Onural's prior Palm Desert spot (The Venue) was repeatedly named 'Best Sushi in Coachella Valley' by Palm Springs Life

entities.name: Wally's Desert Turtle
entities.cuisine_or_type: Classic continental fine dining (Dover sole, rack of lamb, soufflés)
entities.why_mentioned: Established 1978 by Wally Botello (founder of the Velvet Turtle chain); three-generation family operation; longtime celebrity haunt (Hopes, Sinatra, the Fords, Netanyahu, Tom Hanks); featured in Palm Springs Life history coverage

entities.name: The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente Resort Casino Spa
entities.why_mentioned: Self-described and press-reported as the only Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star rated restaurant in the Coachella Valley; AAA Four Diamond; Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007

entities.name: The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage
entities.why_mentioned: Resort-anchored fine dining at the Ritz-Carlton; Satsuma Japanese Wagyu and in-house dry-aging program (21-65 days); cited in nearly every Coachella Valley steakhouse roundup as the resort-tier flagship

entities.name: Mastro's Steakhouse
entities.why_mentioned: Anchor-reference upscale chain on El Paseo at The Gardens; OpenTable Diners' Choice 2025; nightly live entertainment; consistently named in Coachella Valley best-steakhouse coverage

entities.name: Morgan's in the Desert
entities.why_mentioned: Signature fine-dining venue of the historic 1926 La Quinta Resort & Club; led by chef/restaurateur Jimmy Schmidt; consistently cited on La Quinta and Coachella Valley fine-dining lists

content.text:
Returned 14 entities covering the upper-tier independent and resort-anchored fine-dining landscape across Palm Springs (7), Rancho Mirage (3), Palm Desert (1), La Quinta (1) — with substantive provenance for each (founding dates, James Beard recognition, Michelin listings, Forbes/AAA ratings, historical celebrity associations). Notable patterns worth flagging downstream:

- Three entities make verifiable institutional claims that should be preserved: Workshop Kitchen + Bar (2015 James Beard, Design category — confirmed via Palm Springs Life and James Beard press), The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente (Forbes Four-Star, claimed as only one in CV), Le Vallauris (founded 1974, longest continuously operating high-end French in PS).
- The Bar Cecil / Workshop / Sandfish trio represents the modern Palm Springs chef-driven tier; Le Vallauris / Melvyn's / Wally's Desert Turtle represents the legacy continental tier.
- Resort-anchored fine dining is well-covered: The Edge (Ritz-Carlton), Morgan's (La Quinta Resort), The Steakhouse (Agua Caliente), 4 Saints (Kimpton Rowan).
- I did not include Eddie V's, Sullivan's, Morton's, or Chez Pierre Bistro despite appearing on lists — to stay near the ~12 target I prioritized higher-distinctiveness and stronger-provenance entries. Downstream dedupe/expansion can add them.
